formulas.name: Molar Mass of Coordination Compound
formulas.variables: M = Molar mass of the compound, n = number of atoms of each element, M = atomic mass of each element

formulas.name: Oxidation Number (O.N.) of an Atom in a Coordination Compound
formulas.formula: O.N. = charge on cation + (number of bonds to oxygen or fluorine) - (number of bonds to other electronegative atoms)
formulas.variables: O.N. = Oxidation number, charge on cation = charge on the cation, number of bonds = number of bonds to oxygen or fluorine

formulas.name: Isomerism: Coordination Isomerism
formulas.formula: Coordination isomerism occurs when the cation and anion of a complex ion can be interchanged to produce another complex ion
formulas.variables: Complex ion = a cation and an anion linked by a ligand
formulas.notes: This is a key concept, not a formula, but a way of understanding coordination isomerism.

formulas.name: Molecular Formula of a Coordination Compound
formulas.formula: Molecular formula = Cation formula + Anion formula
formulas.variables: Cation formula = formula of the cation, Anion formula = formula of the anion

formulas.name: Empirical Formula of a Coordination Compound
formulas.formula: Empirical formula = simplest whole number ratio of atoms of each element
formulas.variables: Empirical formula = simplest whole number ratio of atoms of each element

formulas.name: Mole Fraction of a Ligand
formulas.formula: X = (number of moles of ligand / total number of moles of all ligands)
formulas.variables: X = mole fraction, number of moles of ligand = number of moles of the ligand, total number of moles of all ligands = total number of moles of all ligands

formulas.name: Ligand Field Splitting Energy (Δo)
formulas.formula: Δo = (energy of highest energy orbital - energy of lowest energy orbital)
formulas.variables: Δo = Ligand field splitting energy, energy of highest energy orbital = energy of highest energy orbital, energy of lowest energy orbital = energy of lowest energy orbital

formulas.name: Crystal Field Stabilization Energy (CFSE)
formulas.formula: CFSE = (number of electrons in t2g orbitals - number of electrons in eg orbitals) × (Δo / 10)
formulas.variables: CFSE = Crystal field stabilization energy, number of electrons in t2g orbitals = number of electrons in t2g orbitals, number of electrons in eg orbitals = number of electrons in eg orbitals, Δo = Ligand field splitting energy
